all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

title: Financial Highlights for
the Full Year of 2024 
summary: In the most recent financial report for the full year ending December 31, 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $924 million, reflecting a substantial 36% increase from the previous year. This growth was primarily driven by a 35% rise in goods sales, which accounted for $891 million, alongside a remarkable 77% increase in service revenues, totaling $33 million. Despite this revenue growth, the company faced challenges with profitability, as the gross margin plummeted to 3%, down from 15% in 2023. The gross margin for goods sales decreased to 3% from 14%, while service revenues saw a decline to 17% from 36%.

The financial struggles were further highlighted by an operating loss of $786 million, marking a 7% increase year-over-year, and a net loss of $1,107 million, which represented a significant 48% rise compared to the prior year. Non-GAAP measures indicated an adjusted net loss of $1,075 million, a 43% increase, and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $961 million, reflecting a 39% increase in losses. Additionally, the cost of revenues surged by 55% to $895 million, resulting in a gross profit of only $29 million. all_retrieved_content.page_content: In the first half of 2024, technology-related revenue began to scale as Lotus Technology started to provide intelligent driving solutions and R&D services to multiple leading automotive brands. As the fleet of models equipped with state-of-the-art hardware continues to expand, paid software subscriptions represent a potential growth driver in the future. The Chapman Bespoke service, which provides customer personalization, design customizations, limited collection editions, and one-off models, was also launched to cater to growing demand. Deliveries of bespoke models are expected to further boost Lotus Technology’s luxury positioning and contribute to the company’s long-term premium gross margin. After assessment of the evolving market conditions and uncertainties posed by new tariff policies in the US and EU, Lotus Technology has revised its delivery target for 2024 to 12,000 units. Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

title: Gross profit and gross margin 
summary: The financial analysis for the years 2022 to 2024 reveals a significant decline in gross profit and gross margin, particularly in 2024. Gross profit dropped to $29.6 million from $102.2 million in 2023, marking a staggering decrease of 71%. Correspondingly, the gross margin fell from 15.0% in 2023 to just 3.2% in 2024. This downturn is primarily attributed to a substantial decline in vehicle sales, which fell by $78.3 million due to a strategic shift from direct sales to a dealer model in the UK and European markets, alongside inventory destocking amid economic uncertainty. The reduction in gross margin was exacerbated by increased discounts and rebates offered to dealers and customers.

Operating expenses also underwent notable changes during this period. Research and development expenses decreased by $93.9 million, reflecting a reduction in activities related to specific vehicle models. Selling and marketing expenses saw a slight decline, driven by cost efficiencies and the transition to a dealer model. Conversely, general and administrative expenses surged by $82.9 million, largely due to increased payroll and professional service costs associated with expanding operations in Europe. 

Despite these challenges, government grants received nearly doubled, providing some financial relief. Overall, total operating expenses for 2024 were reduced slightly compared to 2023, but the company still reported an operational loss of $786.3 million, an increase from the previous year's loss of $735.9 million. This comprehensive overview highlights the critical shifts in financial performance and operational strategies during this period.

summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: •  Projected vehicle related revenue: 
summary: Lotus Tech is projecting significant growth in vehicle sales, anticipating to exceed 73,000 units by 2025, which represents a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 185% from 2023 to 2025. This growth is primarily driven by the sales of its models, including the Eletre, Emeya, Emira, and Evija. The company expects to sell approximately 9,000 vehicles in 2023, with a notable sales distribution from China (55%), Europe (30%), and the US (3%). By 2024, sales are projected to rise to around 47,000 vehicles, and by 2025, to over 73,000, with a more balanced contribution from key markets.

Financially, Lotus Tech anticipates a rise in gross profit margins from an estimated 8%–10% in 2023 to 21%–23% by 2025, despite a downward revision in initial gross margin estimates due to increased raw material costs. The company also expects to achieve a positive EBITDA margin exceeding 4% by 2025, driven by improved gross profit margins and reduced operating expenses. However, the company faces challenges, including potential delays in vehicle deliveries due to customs issues and the European Commission's anti-subsidy investigation affecting pricing strategies in Europe.

Lotus Tech's financial projections include securing $223 million from the Trust Account and an additional $500 million through fundraising, but there are risks associated with meeting these financial targets. The company has indicated a cash outflow exceeding $1 billion from operating and investing activities in 2023, highlighting the need for careful financial management moving forward.

Overall, Lotus Tech's projections reflect a strong growth trajectory in the luxury battery electric vehicle market, with a focus on expanding sales and improving profitability, while also navigating potential market and operational challenges.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 27. REVENUES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. The company's revenues are segmented into sales to third parties and related parties, providing insights into the performance of each area. In 2024, Lotus reported total revenues of $924.3 million, a significant increase from $679.0 million in 2023 and just $9.6 million in 2022.

The company's standout performance was in the sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, which generated $627.7 million in 2024, up from $464.2 million in 2023, with no revenue recorded in 2022. Sales of sports cars also rose to $211.8 million in 2024, compared to $183.2 million the previous year. Total sales of goods to third parties reached $872.9 million in 2024, while related party sales contributed an additional $18.2 million, bringing the subtotal for total goods sold to $891.1 million.

Service revenues presented a mixed picture, with R&D services to third parties declining to $1.2 million, while other services increased to $7.7 million. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational Benchmarking 
summary: The LCAA Board conducted a comprehensive analysis of Lotus Tech's financial metrics, including estimated revenue growth, gross margins, and EBITDA margins, in relation to comparable luxury and high-performance automotive brands. This evaluation utilized Wall Street analyst consensus forecasts for the years 2023 to 2025, based on publicly available data as of January 30, 2023.

Lotus Tech is projected to achieve an impressive revenue compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 90% from 2023 to 2025, with gross margins expected to range from 18.0% to 20.0% in 2024 and 21.0% to 23.0% in 2025. The EBITDA margin is anticipated to exceed 5.0% by 2025. In comparison, other brands show varied performance: Tesla is expected to have a CAGR of 28.4% and strong margins; Lucid forecasts a high CAGR of 125.7% but struggles with negative margins; Polestar and NIO project moderate growth with low margins; while established brands like Ferrari and Porsche maintain robust margins but lower growth rates.

Additionally, the analysis included an evaluation of Lotus Tech's estimated enterprise value-to-sales (EV/Sales) multiples, which are projected to be significantly lower than those of its competitors. For 2024 and 2025, Lotus Tech's multiples are estimated at approximately 0.9x and 0.6x, respectively. In contrast, Tesla leads with multiples of 7.2x and 5.6x, while Ferrari exhibits the highest multiples at 8.4x and 7.7x. Overall, Lotus Tech's lower projected multiples suggest a potential undervaluation compared to its established peers in the automotive sector, despite its promising growth trajectory.

title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial projections for 2023 to 2025, including revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates, to assist in their due diligence process prior to the approval of a business combination. These projections were updated in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." It is important to note that these projections were not prepared in accordance with SEC guidelines or GAAP and were intended for internal use, reflecting management's expectations and assumptions about future performance, which may be subject to significant uncertainties.

The Financial Projections indicate a strong growth trajectory, with projected revenues for 2023 ranging from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion, increasing to $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and reaching $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion by 2025. Gross profit is expected to rise from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to $1.8 billion to $2.0 billion by 2025, while gross margins are projected to improve from 14.0%–16.0% in 2023 to 21.0%–23.0% in 2025.

Lotus Tech anticipates selling over 76,000 vehicles by 2025, driven by several key models, including the Eletre and Type 133. The company expects significant contributions from vehicle sales, technology-related revenue, and ecosystem services. However, the projections carry inherent risks, including potential delays in vehicle launches and market dynamics, which could lead to discrepancies between projected and actual results.

Lotus Tech emphasizes that the Financial Projections should not be relied upon as definitive guidance, as they are speculative and subject to numerous uncertainties. The company does not guarantee the accuracy of these forecasts and will not reference them in future reports post-business combination. The projections were prepared by management and have not been audited by KPMG Huazhen LLP, which only provided assurance on historical financial information. Overall, while the projections suggest a promising outlook for Lotus Tech, stakeholders are advised to approach them with caution due to the various risks and assumptions involved.

title: Non-GAAP Financial Measures 
summary: The company employs adjusted net loss and adjusted EBITDA as essential non-GAAP metrics to evaluate its operating performance and inform financial and operational strategies. Adjusted net loss excludes share-based compensation expenses from the net loss, while adjusted EBITDA is calculated by subtracting interest expenses, interest income, income tax expenses, depreciation, and share-based compensation from net income. These measures are intended to reveal underlying trends that may be masked by certain expenses, thereby enhancing the understanding of the company's financial health for both management and investors.

In the fiscal year 2023, the company reported a net loss of $750,255 thousand, an increase from $724,563 thousand in 2022 and significantly higher than $110,531 thousand in 2021. The adjusted net loss for 2023 was consistent with the net loss figure, while adjusted EBITDA was negative $693,189 thousand, reflecting escalating operational costs. Interest expenses rose steadily, reaching $10,200 thousand in 2023, while interest income declined to $9,204 thousand. The income tax expense increased to $1,113 thousand in 2023, and depreciation costs varied widely, indicating fluctuations in asset utilization.

Revenue for 2023 surged to $679,008 thousand, driven primarily by sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models and sports cars, which saw remarkable increases in sales volume. The cost of revenues also escalated significantly, resulting in a total cost of $576,827 thousand, largely due to increased production costs associated with higher sales. Gross profit rose to $102,181 thousand, but the gross margin declined from 24.1% in 2022 to 15.0% in 2023, reflecting a shift in sales mix towards lower-margin products.

Operating expenses increased overall, with notable rises in selling and marketing costs, while research and development expenses decreased due to a phase-down in specific model activities. The decline in government grants further impacted the financial landscape, highlighting the need for a comprehensive review of the company's financial metrics. Investors are encouraged to consider these non-GAAP measures alongside GAAP figures for a complete understanding of the company's performance, as the adjusted metrics may not be directly comparable to those of other companies.

title: Cost of revenues 
summary: The analysis of the company's cost of revenues reveals significant growth across various categories over the years 2022 to 2024, driven by increased vehicle sales and service demands, particularly in the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector. 

In 2024, the total cost of revenues reached $894.7 million, a substantial increase from $576.8 million in 2023, marking a rise of $317.9 million or 55.1%. This growth was largely attributed to the cost of goods sold (COGS), which rose from $564.7 million in 2023 to $867.1 million in 2024, reflecting a 53.5% increase. The cost of services also saw a dramatic rise, climbing from $12.1 million in 2023 to $27.7 million in 2024, a staggering increase of 128.9%.

A closer look at the previous year, 2023, shows that the total cost of revenues had already surged from just $7.3 million in 2022, with COGS increasing from $948,000 to $564.7 million, and service costs rising from $6.3 million to $12.1 million. This indicates an extraordinary growth trajectory, particularly in COGS, which experienced a remarkable increase of 59,471.8% from 2022 to 2023.

Despite the impressive growth in gross profit—from $2.3 million in 2022 to $102.2 million in 2023—there was a decline in gross margin, which fell from 24.1% to 15.0%. This decline is attributed to a shift towards higher sales volumes of lower-margin vehicles, overshadowing the profitability of services.

Overall, the data underscores a dynamic shift in the company's cost structure, with a pronounced emphasis on COGS as the primary driver of revenue growth, alongside a significant increase in service-related costs, reflecting the company's expansion and evolving market demands.

It is important to note that these projections were not prepared in accordance with SEC guidelines or GAAP and were intended for internal use, reflecting management's expectations and assumptions about future performance, which may be subject to significant uncertainties.

The Financial Projections indicate a strong growth trajectory, with projected revenues for 2023 ranging from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion, increasing to $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and reaching $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion by 2025. Lotus Tech anticipates selling over 76,000 vehicles by 2025, driven by several key models, including the Eletre and Type 133. Lotus Tech emphasizes that the Financial Projections should not be relied upon as definitive guidance, as they are speculative and subject to numerous uncertainties. title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,180 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.
